title: Octets, Byte Streams, Fragmentation, Buffering, and Partial I/O
description: A parser assumes one Read returns a complete line, converts arbitrary bytes to text too early, loses buffered bytes, and treats a short read or write as end-of-message. Learners cumulatively produce fragment-safe byte-stream reader and writer contract through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Keep protocol data as octets until grammar permits text interpretation and distinguish ASCII tokens, opaque content, and invalid control bytes.
Interpret Read results using both byte count and error, including data with io.EOF, zero-byte reads, and temporary fragmentation.
Complete bounded output across short writes while retaining progress, error, and ownership evidence.
